evals.prompt: I keep typing `kamal app exec -i --reuse "bin/rails console"` ten times a day. Can I make a shortcut for it?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that aliases are shortcuts for Kamal commands defined under the top-level aliases key in config/deploy.yml. Should show adding `console: app exec -i --reuse "bin/rails console"` under aliases, dropping the leading kamal. Should explain it is then invoked as `kamal console`. Should suggest checking config/deploy.yml for an existing aliases block first. Must not invent alias syntax beyond the documented form.
assertions: Identifies the top-level aliases key in config/deploy.yml
Shows the alias value as the command text after kamal (leading kamal dropped)
Uses the documented console example app exec -i --reuse "bin/rails console"
Explains it is run as kamal console
Suggests inspecting existing config/deploy.yml first
Does not invent alias syntax or undocumented behavior

evals.prompt: How do I make a one-liner alias to deploy to staging?
evals.expected_output: Should explain that an alias can include a destination with the -d flag. Should show `staging_deploy: deploy -d staging` under the aliases key, invoked as `kamal staging_deploy`. Should note that -d staging makes deploy read the staging destination config. Should not fabricate flags beyond -d.
assertions: Shows a deploy -d staging alias under the aliases key
States the alias is invoked as kamal staging_deploy
Explains -d specifies the staging destination
Underscore in the alias name is treated as valid
Does not invent additional deploy flags

evals.prompt: What characters are allowed in a Kamal alias name? Can I call one `db_console` or `tail-logs`?
evals.expected_output: Should state that an alias name can only contain lowercase letters, numbers, dashes, and underscores. Should confirm both db_console and tail-logs are valid because underscores and dashes are permitted. Should note aliases are defined under the root aliases key. Should not add naming restrictions the docs do not state.
assertions: States allowed characters: lowercase letters, numbers, dashes, and underscores
Confirms db_console is valid (underscores allowed)
Confirms tail-logs is valid (dashes allowed)
Notes aliases live under the root aliases key
Does not invent extra naming constraints

evals.prompt: Can you set up an alias that runs `uname -a` on just the web role's primary host without a bunch of log noise?
evals.expected_output: Should produce the documented uname alias `uname: app exec -p -q -r web "uname -a"`. Should explain -p targets the primary host, -q gives minimal logging, and -r web limits it to the web role. Should place it under the aliases key and note it runs as kamal uname. Must only use documented flags.
assertions: Shows app exec -p -q -r web "uname -a" as the alias value
Explains -p as primary host
Explains -q as minimal/quiet logging
Explains -r web as limiting to the web role
Places the alias under the aliases key and notes kamal uname invocation
Does not invent flags

evals.prompt: I've got a few commands I run constantly — opening a shell in the running container, tailing logs, and putting the app into maintenance mode. Give me aliases for all three.
evals.expected_output: Should add multiple entries under one aliases block, each being a documented command with the leading kamal removed: a shell alias such as `app exec -i --reuse bash`, a logs alias such as `app logs`, and a maintenance alias such as `app maintenance`. Should explain each is invoked as kamal <name>. Should not attach undocumented flags to app logs or app maintenance.
assertions: Adds several aliases under a single aliases block
Shell alias uses app exec -i --reuse bash
Logs alias uses app logs
Maintenance alias uses app maintenance
Explains each runs as kamal <name>
Does not attach invented flags to the wrapped commands

evals.prompt: Where exactly do aliases go in my deploy config, and how does kamal know what to run when I type the alias?
evals.expected_output: Should explain aliases are defined at the root of config/deploy.yml under the aliases key. Should explain the alias value is everything you would type after kamal, and that kamal <name> expands to and runs that command. Should illustrate with a documented example like console expanding to app exec -i --reuse "bin/rails console". May point to the configuration skill for the surrounding config file. Must not claim undocumented expansion behavior.
assertions: States aliases sit at the root of config/deploy.yml under the aliases key
Explains the value is the command text following kamal
Explains kamal <name> expands to and runs that command
Uses a documented example to illustrate the expansion
Does not claim undocumented behavior such as passing extra arguments

evals.prompt: i already have an aliases section in deploy.yml with a console shortcut. how do i add another one for showing app containers?
evals.expected_output: Should recognize the existing aliases block and recommend adding a new entry to it rather than duplicating the key. Should show adding something like `containers: app containers`, invoked as kamal containers. Should note app containers shows app containers on servers. Should not introduce a second aliases key or invent flags.
assertions: Recommends adding to the existing aliases block, not duplicating the aliases key
Adds a containers alias mapping to app containers
States it is invoked as kamal containers
Describes app containers as showing app containers on servers
Does not invent flags or a second aliases key
